action.reasoning: Observation: current best is the original 3-epoch ConvNeXt checkpoint with flip TTA (0.7260); the 10-epoch run degraded and validation curves suggest high seed/augmentation variance. Analysis: the same short-training recipe is validated and cheap enough, while independent seeds can produce complementary errors; averaging logits from multiple strong short-run checkpoints is likely to improve balanced accuracy without changing the model architecture. Decision: train three additional 3-epoch ConvNeXt checkpoints with different seeds, build an ensemble checkpoint including the existing best plus the new runs, and submit it using the existing ensemble-capable predict.py.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 3 --seed 7 --out checkpoints/convnext_seed7_ep3.pt && python train.py --epochs 3 --seed 123 --out checkpoints/convnext_seed123_ep3.pt && python train.py --epochs 3 --seed 999 --out checkpoints/convnext_seed999_ep3.pt && python make_ensemble.py --inputs checkpoints/best.pt checkpoints/convnext_seed7_ep3.pt checkpoints/convnext_seed123_ep3.pt checkpoints/convnext_seed999_ep3.pt --out checkpoints/ensemble4_ep3.pt

training_complete best_val_balanced_accuracy=0.7220 checkpoint=checkpoints/convnext_seed123_ep3.pt
training_complete best_val_balanced_accuracy=0.7290 checkpoint=checkpoints/convnext_seed999_ep3.pt
saved ensemble checkpoints/ensemble4_ep3.pt with 4 members
